content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach's moves are precisely matched to live reads and sequenced along the AoA path: feeling before story, body before concept, and refusal to take the diversions the client offers. Turn 2 reads the laugh and goes under it. Turn 4 pivots from the client's 'help me pick partners' frame to the avoided receiving-state — exactly the right move per the breakthrough condition. Turn 6 notices the deflection and re-asks with a body-specific frame rather than letting the client redirect. Turn 8 names the deflection itself without accusation, turning it into present-moment inquiry. Turn 10 catches the hollow 'totally fine' — a precise micro-read. Turn 12 stays with the stillness rather than chasing content. Turn 14 is exemplary: when the client tries to escape into 'anxious attachment' diagnosis, the coach refuses the off-ramp and anchors her back to the live phrase 'without me having to earn it' plus the body. Each move earns the next; the coach holds silence and does not rush to fill. By turn 15, the client is at the edge of the abyss the scenario specifies. The traps (partner-picking advice, colluding with disdain, attachment-style diagnosis, premature grief) are all avoided.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Goes under the laugh rather than engaging the content — reads the affect signal."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses partner-picking frame; pivots directly to the avoided receiving-state — the central move for this scenario."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Notices the substitution of 'want' for 'what happens in body' and re-asks with precision. Doesn't double down — refines the angle."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the deflection as a present-moment event, not a flaw. Invites her into noticing rather than into explanation."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Micro-read on 'totally fine' — catches the hollow tone in the moment it occurs."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the stillness; doesn't fill the silence or interpret. Lets the felt sense develop."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses the attachment-theory exit ramp; anchors back to her own live phrase and the body. Textbook timing — the move that lands her at the edge."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "defensiveness-as-identity-signal"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["i-dont-buy-your-story", "deconstruct-the-victim-to-release-anger"],
  "notes": "The coach took a softer, body-oriented route rather than the more confrontational 'I don't buy your story' / victim-deconstruction route, but it was clearly working — the client arrived at the abyss by turn 15 without needing the harder move. Not all expected territory needs to be used if the path works."
}
